title: urm split
text: urm split Creates one or more archive files based on object, library, and language. urm { Switches } split ArchiveFile | $RSO { Options } Switches /asn /dir $log /plg /pri /who Parameters ArchiveFile —existing UAR to split into separate UARs $RSO —use the resources output path (as specified by the $RSO logical) as the source of objects to put into UARs. Options —settings that specify the type of objects to package, and the location of the resulting UAR files. Options Options Description -components Put application components and startup shells from the specified source into components.uar . -global Put global objects from the specified source into archive files. Menus, messages, and formats into Library @ Lan .uar Glyphs into Library @ Lan @gly.uar Other global objects into Library .uar -references Put descriptors, signatures, and DTD references from the specified source into references.uar . -objects Put components, global objects, and descriptors from the specified source into archive files; combines -components , -global , and -references . -path= ComponentPath Put compiled components located in the specified directory into an archive file. -noUpdate Display the operations and their results, but do not actually perform the operation. Description The split operation creates one or more archive files based on library and language. Table 1. Archive Files Created by urm split Object Type File Name Descriptors, signatures, and DTD references references.uar Glyphs Library @ Lan @gly.uar Menus, messages, and formats Library @ Lan .uar Other global objects Library .uar Components and startup shells components.uar Was this topic helpful?
